---
title: "Accuracy study - Compare threshold methods"
date: "Compiled at `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', tz = 'UTC')` UTC"
output:
  github_document: default
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
always_allow_html: true
params:
  name: "04_thresh_methods" # change if you rename file
---

```{r here, message=FALSE}
here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "8c524a7c-7a6c-474e-8519-34397abf40d9")
```

```{r directories}
# create or *empty* the target directory, used to write this file's data: 
#projthis::proj_create_dir_target(params$name, clean = TRUE)

# function to get path to target directory: path_target("sample.csv")
path_target <- projthis::proj_path_target(params$name)

# function to get path to previous data: path_source("00-import", "sample.csv")
path_source <- projthis::proj_path_source(params$name)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = c("png", "cairo_pdf"),
  fig.ext = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.width = 10, 
  fig.height = 6, 
  dpi = 200,
  fig.path = paste0('figures/', params, "/sim_ttest_thresh_"),
  message = FALSE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  echo = FALSE)
```

```{r packages}
library(conflicted)
library(dplyr); conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter, dplyr::select)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(progressr)
library(future)
library(furrr)
library(readr)
library(rlang) # For global plot theme
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

conflicts_prefer(base::intersect)
```

```{r}
# Global parameters
cores <- 8
set.seed(123456)
```


## Load permApprox functions

```{r permapprox-load}
proj_path <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("README.md"))

# Load permApprox functions
permApprox_path <- file.path(proj_path, "functions/permApprox_eps_fct/R")
permApprox_functions <- list.files(permApprox_path, pattern = "\\.R$",
                                   full.names = TRUE)
invisible(sapply(permApprox_functions, source))

# Load epsilon functions
eps_rule_path <- file.path(proj_path, 
                           "functions/permApprox_eps_fct/slls_eps_rule.R")
source(eps_rule_path)
```

## Method registry, file helpers, and per-method runner

```{r helpers}
# Root where generated data are stored
path_data <- path_source("01_data_gen")

path_acc <- file.path(path_data, "sim_accuracy_data")
path_err <- file.path(path_data, "sim_error_data")

stopifnot(dir.exists(path_acc), dir.exists(path_err))

parse_acc_file <- function(fn) {
  # sim_acc_n{n}_d{d}_nperm{nperm}.rds
  m <- stringr::str_match(basename(fn), 
                          "^sim_acc_n(\\d+)_d([0-9.]+)_nperm(\\d+)\\.rds$")
  tibble(n = as.integer(m[,2]),
         d = as.numeric(m[,3]),
         n_perm = as.integer(m[,4]),
         file = fn)
}

list_acc <- function() {
  fs <- list.files(path_acc, pattern = "\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
  purrr::map_dfr(fs, parse_acc_file) %>% arrange(n, d, n_perm)
}
```

### General method registry

```{r}
# --- General method registry (threshold study) --------------------------------
# Edit here to add/remove methods or change labels/controls.
# Labels must match the plotting color names.
methods_registry <- list(
  ttest = list(
    label  = "t-test",
    engine = "ttest",
    params = list()
  ),

  gpd_ftr = list(
    label  = "GPD(FTR)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "ftr",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 10,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  ),

  gpd_rob_ftr = list(
    label  = "GPD(robFTR)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "rob_ftr",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 10,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  ),
  
  gpd_rpc = list(
    label  = "GPD(RPC)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "rpc",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 1,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  ),
  
  gpd_rpc_min = list(
    label  = "GPD(RPC-Min)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "rpc_min",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 1,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  ),
  
  gpd_fwd_stop = list(
    label  = "GPD(FwdStop)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "fwd_stop",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 1,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  ),

  gpd_gof_cp = list(
    label  = "GPD(GOF-CP)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "gof_cp",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 1,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  ),

  gpd_shape_var = list(
    label  = "GPD(Shape-Var)",
    engine = "gpd",
    params = list(
      gpd_ctrl = make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method    = "LME",
        tol           = 1e-7,
        include_obs   = FALSE,
        constraint    = "support_at_obs",
        thresh_method = "shape_var",
        exceed0       = 0.25,
        exceed0_min   = 250,
        exceed_min    = 10,
        thresh_step   = 1,
        gof_test      = "ad",
        gof_alpha     = 0.05,
        cores         = 1,
        verbose       = FALSE
      )
    )
  )
)

# Labels (used later for factor order)
method_labels <- vapply(methods_registry, `[[`, "", "label")
```

### Engines

```{r}
.engine_ttest <- function(lst_panels, n, d, n_perm, study_str, label, 
                          method_key, pi1 = NA_real_) {
  purrr::imap_dfr(lst_panels, function(panel, rep_id) {
    n_test <- nrow(panel)
    stopifnot(ncol(panel) == n_perm + 2)
    colnames(panel) <- c(as.character(seq_len(n_perm)), "tobs", "pttest")
    tibble(
      study    = gsub("^acc_", "", study_str),
      n, d, n_perm, pi1,
      rep      = rep_id,
      test_idx = seq_len(n_test),
      method   = label,
      pval     = as.numeric(panel[, "pttest"]),
      meth_used = NA_character_,
      n_exceed  = NA_integer_
    )
  })
}

.engine_gpd <- function(lst_panels, n, d, n_perm, study_str, label, 
                        method_key, params, pi1 = NA_real_) {
  gpd_ctrl <- params$gpd_ctrl
  purrr::imap_dfr(lst_panels, function(panel, rep_id) {
    n_test <- nrow(panel)
    stopifnot(ncol(panel) == n_perm + 2)
    colnames(panel) <- c(as.character(seq_len(n_perm)), "tobs", "pttest")
    
    perm_mat <- t(as.matrix(panel[, seq_len(n_perm), drop = FALSE]))  # (B x m)
    tobs_vec <- as.numeric(panel[, "tobs"])
    
    fit <- compute_p_values(
      obs_stats     = tobs_vec,
      perm_stats    = perm_mat,
      eps_fct       = eps_slls,
      sampsize      = n,
      alternative   = "two_sided",
      method        = "gpd",
      fit_thresh    = 1,
      gpd_ctrl      = gpd_ctrl,
      adjust_method = "none"
    )

    tibble(
      study    = gsub("^acc_", "", study_str),
      n, d, n_perm, pi1,
      rep      = rep_id,
      test_idx = seq_len(n_test),
      method   = label,
      pval     = fit$p_unadjusted,
      meth_used = fit$method_used,
      n_exceed  = fit$gpd_fit$n_exceed
    )
  })
}

.engine_gpd <- function(lst_panels, n, d, n_perm, study_str, label, 
                        method_key, params, pi1 = NA_real_) {
  
  gpd_ctrl <- params$gpd_ctrl
  
  # preallocate list for results (faster than growing a data frame)
  res_list <- vector("list", length(lst_panels))
  
  for (rep_id in seq_along(lst_panels)) {
    
    cat("Replication:", rep_id, "\n")  # or print(rep_id)
    
    panel <- lst_panels[[rep_id]]
    
    n_test <- nrow(panel)
    stopifnot(ncol(panel) == n_perm + 2)
    
    colnames(panel) <- c(as.character(seq_len(n_perm)), "tobs", "pttest")
    
    perm_mat <- t(as.matrix(panel[, seq_len(n_perm), drop = FALSE]))  # (B x m)
    tobs_vec <- as.numeric(panel[, "tobs"])
    
    fit <- compute_p_values(
      obs_stats     = tobs_vec,
      perm_stats    = perm_mat,
      eps_fct       = eps_slls,
      sampsize      = n,
      alternative   = "two_sided",
      method        = "gpd",
      fit_thresh    = 1,
      gpd_ctrl      = gpd_ctrl,
      adjust_method = "none"
    )
    
    res_list[[rep_id]] <- tibble(
      study    = gsub("^acc_", "", study_str),
      n        = n,
      d        = d,
      n_perm   = n_perm,
      pi1      = pi1,
      rep      = rep_id,
      test_idx = seq_len(n_test),
      method   = label,
      pval     = fit$p_unadjusted,
      meth_used = fit$method_used,
      n_exceed  = fit$gpd_fit$n_exceed
    )
  }
  
  # bind all results like imap_dfr()
  dplyr::bind_rows(res_list)
}

# (Other engines like empirical/custom can be added here)

# --- Dispatcher ---------------------------------------------------------------
run_method_engine <- function(method_key, def, lst_panels, n, d, n_perm, 
                              study_str, pi1 = NA_real_) {
  engine <- def$engine
  label  <- def$label
  params <- def$params %||% list()

  switch(engine,
    ttest = .engine_ttest(lst_panels, n, d, n_perm, study_str, label, 
                          method_key, pi1),
    gpd   = .engine_gpd(lst_panels, n, d, n_perm, study_str, label, 
                        method_key, params, pi1),
    stop(sprintf("Unknown engine: %s", engine))
  )
}
```

### Output path builders

```{r}
path_results <- path_target()

.method_out_file <- function(study_str, method_key, n, d, n_perm, 
                             pi1 = NA_real_) {
  dir.create(file.path(path_results, study_str, method_key), 
             recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  if (is.na(pi1)) {
    file.path(path_results, study_str, method_key,
              sprintf("%s_pvals_%s_n%d_d%.1f_nperm%d.rds", study_str, 
                      method_key, n, d, n_perm))
  } else {
    file.path(path_results, study_str, method_key,
              sprintf("%s_pvals_%s_n%d_d%.1f_nperm%d_pi1%.3f.rds", 
                      study_str, method_key, n, d, n_perm, pi1))
  }
}
```

## ACCURACY

### Compute p-values and save

```{r accuracy-run, eval=FALSE}
# --- ACCURACY: compute per method, each into its own file ---------------------
acc_grid <- list_acc() %>% filter(n_perm <= 1000)

handlers("txtprogressbar")
plan(multisession, workers = cores)

base_out <- file.path(path_results, "accuracy")
dir.create(base_out, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

with_progress({
  p <- progressor(steps = nrow(acc_grid) * length(methods_registry))

  furrr::future_walk(
    split(acc_grid, seq_len(nrow(acc_grid))),
    function(row) {
      row <- as_tibble(row)
      n      <- row$n[[1]]
      d      <- row$d[[1]]
      n_perm <- row$n_perm[[1]]
      f_in   <- row$file[[1]]
      study_str <- "accuracy"

      lst <- readRDS(f_in)
      #lst <- lst[1:50]

      purrr::iwalk(methods_registry, function(def, key) {
        f_out <- .method_out_file(study_str, key, n, d, n_perm)
        if (!file.exists(f_out)) {
          res <- run_method_engine(
            method_key = key, def = def,
            lst_panels = lst, n = n, d = d, n_perm = n_perm,
            study_str = study_str
          )
          saveRDS(res, f_out)
        }
        p()
      })
    },
    .options = furrr::furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
  )
})

plan(sequential)
```

### Collect & reshape

```{r accuracy-collect}
# --- Collect all method files for ACCURACY -----------------------------------
acc_method_root <- file.path(path_results, "accuracy")
acc_files <- list.files(acc_method_root, pattern = "\\.rds$", 
                        recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

acc_res <- purrr::map_dfr(acc_files, readRDS) %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method, levels = unname(method_labels)))
```

```{r}
## Rename methods
acc_res <- acc_res %>%
  mutate(method = dplyr::recode(
    method,
    "t-test"         = "t-test",
    "GPD(FTR)"       = "FTR",
    "GPD(robFTR)"    = "robFTR",
    "GPD(RPC)"       = "RPC",
    "GPD(RPC-Min)"   = "RPC-Min",
    "GPD(FwdStop)"   = "FwdStop",
    "GPD(GOF-CP)"    = "GOF-CP",
    "GPD(Shape-Var)" = "Shape-Var"
  ))

method_order <- c(
  "t-test",
  "FTR",
  "FwdStop",
  "robFTR",
  "GOF-CP",
  "RPC",
  "RPC-Min",
  "Shape-Var"
)

acc_res <- acc_res %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method, levels = method_order))
```


```{r}
# What the plotting/tables expect:
acc_long <- acc_res %>%
  select(study, n, d, n_perm, rep, test_idx, method, pval)

# long DF for exceedance counts
acc_long_exceed <- acc_res %>%
  # exceedances only make sense for GPD methods; t-test is NA
  filter(method != "t-test") %>%
  select(study, n, d, n_perm, rep, test_idx, method, n_exceed)
```

```{r colors}
# Okabe Ito palette (colorblind-friendly)
col_palette <- c(black       = "black", 
                 lightgray   = "gray70",
                 gray        = "#6C6C6C",
                 darkgray    = "gray30",
                 yellow      = "#F0E442",
                 lightorange = "#E69F00",
                 darkorange  = "#D55E00",
                 lightblue   = "#56B4E9", 
                 darkblue    = "#0072B2",
                 lightgreen  = "#B2DF8A", # no okabe ito
                 green       = "#009E73",
                 green2      = "#33A02C", # no okabe ito
                 pink        = "#CC79A7")

my_cols <- c(
  "t-test"    = "#6C6C6C",
  "FTR"       = "#56B4E9",
  "FwdStop"   = "#009E73",
  "robFTR"    = "#0072B2",
  "GOF-CP"    = "#CC79A7",
  "RPC"       = "#E69F00",
  "RPC-Min"   = "#F0E442",
  "Shape-Var" = "#D55E00"
)

theme_params <- list(
  axis.text       = element_text(size = 12),
  axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
  axis.title      = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.text     = element_text(size = 14),
  legend.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
  plot.title      = element_text(size = 16)
)
```

### P-values by n (boxplots only)

```{r}
## Prepare data frame for plotting (global zero replacement)

# Smallest nonzero p across everything shown in the plot
min_nonzero_global <- suppressWarnings(min(acc_long$pval[acc_long$pval > 0], 
                                           na.rm = TRUE))
if (!is.finite(min_nonzero_global)) min_nonzero_global <- 1e-320

# Choose a floor (a few orders smaller than the min nonzero)
p_floor <- min_nonzero_global * 1e-3

acc_long <- acc_long %>%
  mutate(pval_plot = ifelse(pval == 0, p_floor, pval))

# Y-axis breaks + label "0" at the floor
base_breaks <- c(1e-1, 10^seq(-20, -300, by = -20))
yrange_max  <- max(acc_long$pval_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
brks <- sort(unique(c(p_floor, base_breaks[base_breaks <= yrange_max])))
```


```{r pvals_by_n_boxplot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
df_plot <- acc_long %>%
  filter(n_perm == 1000, d == 1)

# Accuracy boxplot using the replaced values
gg_acc <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = factor(n), y = pval_plot, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.size = 0.3, width = 0.75,
    position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.2)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual("Method", values = my_cols) +
  scale_y_log10(
    breaks = brks,
    labels = function(x) {
      ifelse(x < p_floor * 1.1, "0",
             paste0("1e", log10(x)))
    })+  
  labs(title = "", x = "n", y = expression(p)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    !!!theme_params,
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

gg_acc
```

### Plot function (p-values / ratios)

```{r}
# Plot function
plot_permapprox <- function(acc_long,
                            type = c("pval", "ratio"),
                            x = c("n", "d", "n_perm"),
                            xlab = NULL,
                            fixed = list(),            # e.g. list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000)
                            my_cols,
                            theme_params = list(),
                            zero_floor_factor = 1e-3,  # p-values: 0 -> min_nonzero * factor
                            y_min = 1e-300,
                            y_max = 1e+300,
                            y_step_size = 20,
                            jitter_width = 0.18,
                            dodge_width = 0.75,
                            show_zero_labels = TRUE,   # annotate "0s=…"
                            legend_point_size = 2.5) {
  stopifnot(all(c("n","d","n_perm","method","pval") %in% names(acc_long)))

  type <- match.arg(type)
  x    <- match.arg(x)

  # ---- Decide needed fixed filters based on x ----
  # default anchors if not provided
  defaults <- list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000)
  fixed <- modifyList(defaults, fixed)

  # build filter
  filt <- dplyr::quo(TRUE)
  if (x != "n")      filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & n      == !!fixed$n)
  if (x != "d")      filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & d      == !!fixed$d)
  if (x != "n_perm") filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & n_perm == !!fixed$n_perm)

  df <- acc_long %>%
    dplyr::filter(!!filt)

  if (!nrow(df)) 
    stop("No rows after filtering. Adjust 'fixed = list(...)' to existing settings.")

  # order methods to match colors provided
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method = factor(method, levels = names(my_cols)))

  # helper for legend guides
  guide_bump <- list(
    ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = FALSE,
                          override.aes = list(size = legend_point_size)),
    ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = FALSE,
                          override.aes = list(size = legend_point_size))
  )

  # ---------------------------
  # P-VALUE PLOT
  # ---------------------------
  if (type == "pval") {
    # compute global floor for zeros (across the plotted subset)
    min_nonzero <- suppressWarnings(min(df$pval[df$pval > 0], na.rm = TRUE))
    if (!is.finite(min_nonzero)) min_nonzero <- 1e-320
    p_floor <- min_nonzero * zero_floor_factor

    df <- df %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        pval_plot = dplyr::if_else(pval == 0, p_floor, pval, missing = pval)
      )

    # jitter points: use all values; boxplots: exclude zeros to avoid huge IQR
    df_points <- df
    df_boxes  <- dplyr::filter(df, pval > 0)

    # zero counts per x × method
    zero_counts <- df %>%
      dplyr::group_by(.data[[x]], method) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(n_zero = sum(pval == 0, na.rm = TRUE), 
                       .groups = "drop") %>%
      dplyr::filter(n_zero > 0)

    # y-scale breaks + "0" tick only if there are zeros present
    has_zero <- any(df$pval == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    # y_min <- min(df$pval_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
    # y_max <- max(df$pval_plot, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # A tick every 20 powers
    base_breaks <- c(1e-2, 10^seq(-20, -300, by = -y_step_size))
    brks <- base_breaks[base_breaks <= (y_max) & 
                          base_breaks >= (y_min)]
    
    if (has_zero) brks <- sort(unique(c(p_floor, brks)))
    
    y_labs <- function(v) {
      if (has_zero) {
        ifelse(v < p_floor * 1.1, "0", paste0("1e", as.integer(log10(v))))
      } else {
        paste0("1e", as.integer(log10(v)))
      }
    }
    
    p <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]))) +
      # jitter (colored)
      geom_point(
        data = df_points,
        aes(y = pval_plot, color = method, fill = method),
        size = 0.9, alpha = 0.9,
        position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = jitter_width, 
                                        dodge.width = dodge_width)
      ) +
      # box (transparent outline) on non-zeros only
      geom_boxplot(
        data = df_boxes,
        aes(y = pval_plot, group = interaction(method, .data[[x]])),
        fill = NA, color = "black", width = 0.65,
        outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
        show.legend = FALSE
      ) +
      # annotate zero counts (optional)
      {if (show_zero_labels && nrow(zero_counts)) geom_text(
        data = zero_counts,
        aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]), 
            y = if (length(brks)) min(brks) else p_floor,
            label = paste0("0s=", n_zero), color = method, group = method),
        position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
        vjust = 1.6, size = 3.5, inherit.aes = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE
      )}
    
    p <- p +
      scale_fill_manual("Method:", 
                        values = my_cols,
                        labels = setNames(
                          lapply(names(my_cols), function(m) {
                            #if (m == "robFTR") bquote(bold("robFTR" * "*")) else m
                            m
                          }),
                          names(my_cols)
                        )) +
      scale_color_manual("Method:", 
                         values = my_cols,
                         labels = setNames(
                           lapply(names(my_cols), function(m) {
                             m
                             #if (m == "robFTR") bquote(bold("robFTR" * "*")) else m
                           }),
                           names(my_cols)
                         )) +
      scale_y_log10(breaks = brks, labels = y_labs) +
      labs(title = "", 
           x = ifelse(is.null(xlab), x, xlab), 
           y = expression(p)) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      do.call(theme, c(theme_params, list(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      ))) +
      guides(color = guide_bump[[1]], fill = guide_bump[[2]])

    return(p)
  }

  # ---------------------------
  # RATIO PLOT (method / t-test), non-zero pairs only
  # ---------------------------
  # For ratio, we need tests aligned with t-test baseline
  df_base <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(n, d, n_perm, !!rlang::sym(x), rep, test_idx, method, pval)

  # Count zeros for annotation (exclude t-test)
  zero_counts_r <- df_base %>%
    dplyr::filter(method != "t-test") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data[[x]], method) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(n_zero = sum(pval == 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::filter(n_zero > 0)

  # Wide join to get t-test next to each method
  df_wide <- tidyr::pivot_wider(df_base, names_from = method, values_from = pval)

  methods_to_compare <- intersect(names(my_cols), 
                                  setdiff(unique(acc_long$method), "t-test"))
  if (length(methods_to_compare) == 0) {
    methods_to_compare <- c("GPD(FTR)", "GPD(robFTR)", "GPD(RPC)", "GPD(FwdStop)", 
                            "GPD(GOF-CP)", "GPD(Shape-Var)") # fallback
  }
  
  df_ratio <- df_wide %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = tidyselect::all_of(methods_to_compare),
                        names_to = "method", values_to = "p_method") %>%
    dplyr::rename(p_ttest = `t-test`) %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_ttest > 0, p_method > 0) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      method = factor(method, levels = names(my_cols)),
      ratio  = p_method / p_ttest
    )
  
  if (!nrow(df_ratio)) 
    stop("No non-zero pairs for ratio plot after filtering. ", 
         "Try a different 'fixed' combination.")
  
  r_min <- min(df_ratio$ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  r_max <- max(df_ratio$ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  base_breaks <- sort(c(10^seq(300, -300, by = -y_step_size)))
  # brks_r <- base_breaks[base_breaks <= (r_max * 10^y_step_size) & 
  #                         base_breaks >= (r_min * 10^-y_step_size)]
  brks_r <- base_breaks[base_breaks <= y_max & base_breaks >= y_min]
  
  lab_plain <- function(v) ifelse(v == 1, "1", paste0("1e", as.integer(log10(v))))

  p <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]))) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linewidth = 0.5, linetype = 2, color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(
      data = df_ratio,
      aes(y = ratio, color = method, fill = method),
      size = 0.9, alpha = 0.9,
      position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = jitter_width, dodge.width = dodge_width)
    ) +
    geom_boxplot(
      data = df_ratio,
      aes(y = ratio, group = interaction(method, .data[[x]])),
      fill = NA, color = "black", width = 0.65,
      outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    {if (show_zero_labels && nrow(zero_counts_r)) geom_text(
      data = zero_counts_r,
      aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]), y = min(brks_r),
                   label = paste0("0s=", n_zero),
                   color = method, group = method),
      position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
      vjust = 1.6, size = 3.5, inherit.aes = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE
    )} +
    scale_fill_manual("Method:", 
                      values = my_cols,
                      labels = setNames(
                        lapply(names(my_cols), function(m) {
                          #if (m == "robFTR") bquote(bold("robFTR" * "*")) else m
                          m
                        }),
                        names(my_cols)
                      )) +
    scale_color_manual("Method:", 
                       values = my_cols,
                       labels = setNames(
                         lapply(names(my_cols), function(m) {
                           #if (m == "robFTR") bquote(bold("robFTR" * "*")) else m
                           m
                         }),
                         names(my_cols)
                       )) +
    scale_y_log10(breaks = brks_r, labels = lab_plain) +
    labs(title = "", 
         x = ifelse(is.null(xlab), x, xlab), 
         y = expression(p[method] / p[ttest])) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    do.call(theme, c(theme_params, list(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ))) +
    guides(color = guide_bump[[1]], fill = guide_bump[[2]])

  return(p)
}
```

### Plot function (exceedances)

```{r}
plot_exceedances <- function(acc_long_exceed,
                             x = c("n","d","n_perm"),
                             xlab = NULL,
                             fixed = list(),                 # e.g. list(d=1, n_perm=1000)
                             my_cols,
                             theme_params = list(),
                             jitter_width = 0.18,
                             dodge_width  = 0.75,
                             legend_point_size = 2.5) {

  stopifnot(all(c("n","d","n_perm","method","n_exceed") %in% names(acc_long_exceed)))
  x <- match.arg(x)

  # anchors like in plot_permapprox()
  defaults <- list(n = 100, d = 1, n_perm = 1000)
  fixed <- modifyList(defaults, fixed)

  filt <- rlang::expr(TRUE)
  if (x != "n")      filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & n      == !!fixed$n)
  if (x != "d")      filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & d      == !!fixed$d)
  if (x != "n_perm") filt <- rlang::expr(!!filt & n_perm == !!fixed$n_perm)

  df <- acc_long_exceed %>%
    dplyr::filter(!!filt) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(n_exceed)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method = factor(method, levels = names(my_cols)))

  if (!nrow(df)) stop("No rows after filtering for exceedance plot.")

  # same two-layer structure as your p-value plot
  df_points <- df
  df_boxes  <- df   # keep all (including zeros) for exceedances
  
  # helper for legend guides
  guide_bump <- list(
    ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = FALSE,
                          override.aes = list(size = legend_point_size)),
    ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = FALSE,
                          override.aes = list(size = legend_point_size))
  )
  
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = factor(.data[[x]]))) +
    geom_point(
      data = df_points,
      aes(y = n_exceed, color = method, fill = method),
      size = 0.9, alpha = 0.9,
      position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = jitter_width,
                                      dodge.width  = dodge_width)
    ) +
    geom_boxplot(
      data = df_boxes,
      aes(y = n_exceed, group = interaction(method, .data[[x]])),
      fill = NA, color = "black", width = 0.65,
      outlier.shape = NA,
      position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual("Method:", 
                      values = my_cols,
                      labels = setNames(
                        lapply(names(my_cols), function(m) {
                          #if (m == "robFTR") bquote(bold("robFTR" * "*")) else m
                          m
                        }),
                        names(my_cols)
                      )) +
    scale_color_manual("Method:", 
                       values = my_cols,
                       labels = setNames(
                         lapply(names(my_cols), function(m) {
                           #if (m == "robFTR") bquote(bold("robFTR" * "*")) else m
                           m
                         }),
                         names(my_cols)
                       )) +
    labs(title = "",
         x = ifelse(is.null(xlab), x, xlab),
         y = "number of exceedances") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    do.call(theme, c(theme_params, list(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ))) +
    guides(color = guide_bump[[1]], fill = guide_bump[[2]])
}


```

### P-values by n

Approximated p-values with sample size on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

```{r pvals_by_n_all, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "n",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-130, y_max = 1)
```

### nExceed by n


```{r}
plot_exceedances(acc_long_exceed,
                 x = "n",
                 fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                 my_cols = my_cols,
                 theme_params = theme_params)

```

### Filter out small p-values

To increase readability of the plots, we remove the two extremely small p-value approximations for the RPC method.

### P-values by n

Since the RPC method performs so poorly, we will omit it in the following.

```{r}
acc_long <- acc_long %>%
  filter(pval > 1e-200)
```

```{r pvals_by_n, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "n",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-180, y_max = 1)
```

```{r pvals_by_n_sel, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
p_pvals_by_n <- acc_long %>%
  filter(n %in% c(50, 100, 500, 1000)) %>%
  plot_permapprox(type = "pval", x = "n",
                  fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  y_min = 1e-130, y_max = 1)
print(p_pvals_by_n)
```

### Ratios by n

Ratios ($p_{method}$ / $p_{ttest}$) for non-zero p-values, with sample size on the x axis.

```{r ratios_by_n, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "ratio", x = "n",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-40)
```

```{r ratios_by_n_sel, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
# P-values by n (fix d=1, B=1000)
p_ratios_by_n <- acc_long %>%
  filter(n %in% c(50, 100, 500, 1000)) %>%
  plot_permapprox(type = "ratio", x = "n",
                  fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                  my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                  y_min = 1e-40)
print(p_ratios_by_n)
```

### nExceed by n

```{r nexceed_by_n, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_exceedances(acc_long_exceed, x = "n",
            fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
            my_cols = my_cols,
            theme_params = theme_params)
```

```{r nexceed_by_n_sel, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
p_nexc_by_n <- acc_long_exceed %>% 
  filter(n %in% c(50, 100, 500, 1000)) %>%
  plot_exceedances(x = "n",
            fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
            my_cols = my_cols,
            theme_params = theme_params)
print(p_nexc_by_n)
```

### Combined

```{r combined_pvals_nexceed_by_n, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 5, dpi = 200}
p_ratios_by_n + p_nexc_by_n +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


### P-values by d

Approximated p-values with effect size on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

```{r pvals_by_d, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "d",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_min = 1e-50)
```

### Ratios by d

Ratios ($p_{method}$ / $p_{ttest}$) for non-zero p-values, with effect size on the x axis.

```{r ratios_by_d, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "ratio", x = "d",
                fixed = list(n = 100, n_perm = 1000),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_step_size = 5, y_min = 1e-20)
```

### P-values by B

Approximated p-values with number of permutations on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

```{r pvals_by_B, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "pval", x = "n_perm",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n = 100),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_step_size = 10, y_min = 1e-40)
```

### Ratios by B

Ratios ($p_{method}$ / $p_{ttest}$) for non-zero p-values, with number of permutations on the x axis.

```{r ratios_by_B, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 200}
plot_permapprox(acc_long, type = "ratio", x = "n_perm",
                fixed = list(d = 1, n = 100),
                my_cols = my_cols, theme_params = theme_params,
                y_step_size = 5, y_min = 1e-30)
```



## Files written

These files have been written to the target directory, ```r paste0("data/", params$name)```:

```{r list-files-target}
projthis::proj_dir_info(path_target())
```
